key_insight: A fault-tolerant, literature-grounded LLM pipeline can autonomously move from a large physics corpus to a publishable manuscript while avoiding ungrounded hallucinations by forcing calibration against reproduced references and shared on-disk state across fresh sessions.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a scalable architecture for high-stakes scientific automation: isolate sessions, anchor reasoning to external literature, and insert checkpointed reproduction steps to keep autonomous discovery numerically and scientifically grounded.
method: Map a corpus of 11,083 recent condensed-matter arXiv papers to identify a research direction.
Use 47 fresh-context sessions in six phases with shared on-disk state and 2,162 literature-consultation events.
Ground the pipeline through reproduction checkpoints, first-principles computations, and adversarial review.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports three substantive physics findings, six-phase execution across 47 sessions, and 2,162 literature-consultation events as evidence of end-to-end autonomous but grounded research.
watch_item: The approach still depends on bounded human intervention when reproduction fails, so real-world deployment needs robust failure detection and explicit oversight at calibration checkpoints.
paper_title: Grounded autonomous research: a fault-tolerant LLM pipeline from corpus to manuscript in frontier computational physics
